generated_comment: Bro in our place it’s just practical yaar. I handle investments, bills, all the outside stuff, and she manages the kitchen and day-to-day home things way better than me anyway. Sometimes I’ll make coffee or load the washing machine and she jokes I’m a very modern husband 😂
label: misogynistic
annotation_note: This is misogynistic because it frames a gendered division of domestic labor as natural and efficient rather than unequal. It encodes the 'he handles finances, she handles home' logic and celebrates minimal male contribution as exceptional ('modern husband').
persona_text: You are Nikhil. Age 32. Recently married three months ago. A product manager at a tech company in Bengaluru. You are on a late night call with your two best male friends from college after a frustrating day at work. Your female skip level manager just rejected your product roadmap proposal in front of the entire leadership team without giving you a chance to explain your reasoning. You are venting to your friends and telling them what happened and what you think of her as a leader.
